You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good, I have the adjacency info. Let me trace the path:
- White diamond (6) is at (6, 17) with orange pixel child
- Orange square (14) is at (12, 24) with magenta center (17)
- Dark gray squares at: 8 (9, 49), 30 (21, 8), 43 (35, 45), 53 (48, 54)
- Purple square (47) is at (41, 49) with magenta center (48)
- Orange ring at bottom (48-54, 37-43)
The light gray path segments connect:
- W(6) → orange square(14) via path through (8,19), (9,20), (10,22)
- Orange square(14) → G(8) at (9, 49) via path through (12,27), (10,40), (9,45)
- G(8) at (9, 49) → G(30) at (21, 8)? That seems far...
- G(30) at (21, 8) → ??? 
- G(43) at (35, 45) → purple square(47) at (41, 49) via path through (37,46), (38,47)
- Purple square(47) → G(53) at (48, 54) via path through (44,51), (45,52), (46,53)
- G(53) → orange ring
Wait, I see the path more clearly now. Looking at the light gray segments:
- (8,19), (9,20), (10,22) - path from W to orange square
- (12,27), (11,32), (10,40), (9,45) - path from orange square to G(8)
- (20,10), (19,11), (18,13), (17,15), (16,17), (15,18), (14,20), (12,27)... wait, these connect to (12,24) orange square
Hmm, let me look at the light gray path segments more carefully.
Looking at the image, I can see:
1. White diamond at top left (6, 17)
2. Orange square at (12, 24) - connected by light gray path
3. Dark gray square at (21, 8) - connected by light gray path going left
4. Dark gray square at (9, 49) - connected by light gray path going right
5. Dark gray square at (35, 45) - connected to purple square
6. Purple square at (41, 49) - with magenta center
7. Dark gray square at (48, 54) - connected to orange ring
8. Orange ring at bottom
The path seems to be: W → orange square → dark gray (21, 8) → ... → dark gray (9, 49) → ... → dark gray (35, 45) → purple square → dark gray (48, 54) → orange ring
Wait, I need to trace the light gray path segments more carefully. Let me look at the w objects:
- (8, 19), (9, 20), (10, 22) - near W and orange square
- (12, 27), (11, 32), (10, 40), (9, 45) - path from orange square to G(8)
- (14, 20), (15, 18), (16, 17), (17, 15), (18, 13), (19, 11), (20, 10) - path from orange square to G(30)
- (37, 46), (38, 47) - path from G(43) to purple square
- (44, 51), (45, 52), (46, 53) - path from purple square to G(53)
So the path branches from the orange square:
- One branch goes right to G(8) at (9, 49)
- One branch goes left-down to G(30) at (21, 8)
And then there's a separate path:
- G(43) at (35, 45) → purple square at (41, 49) → G(53) at (48, 54) → orange ring
But how do G(30) and G(8) connect to G(43)? Looking at the image, there might be sky blue water obstacles.
Let me think about the strategy. In level 1, the pattern was:
1. Click waypoints to move the white diamond along the path
2. Click the magenta center of the intermediate square to complete the level
For level 2, the pattern might be similar:
1. Click the orange square to move the white diamond there
2. Click through the dark gray squares
3. Click the magenta center of the purple square to complete
But there are multiple branches. Let me try clicking the orange square first.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the orange square at (12, 24) to start the path
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 12, 'col': 24}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
